spans.content: Consider the eigenvalue equation for a unitary operator
an algorithm to find the phase
, assuming that black boxes (circuits) are available
The procedure of phase estimation uses two registers: the first register contains t
qubits, each in the initial state
. The number t will depend on:
- the accuracy we want to reach in the estimation of the phase
- the probability of success of the algorithm
The second register contains initially the eigenstate
number of qubits necessary to encode the eigenstate.
The circuit for the phase estimation is given in Fig. 21.1. The e↵ect of the
sequence of controlled-U gates on the state

\left| j _ {1} \dots j _ {t} \right\rangle | u \rangle \longrightarrow \left| j _ {1} \dots j _ {t} \right\rangle U ^ {2 ^ {t - 1} j _ {1}} \dots U ^ {2 ^ {0} j _ {t}} | u \rangle = | j \rangle U ^ {j} | u \rangle \tag {21.2}
\left| j \right\rangle \left| u \right\rangle \longrightarrow \left| j \right\rangle e ^ {2 \pi i \varphi j} \left| u \right\rangle \tag {21.3}
The first register exits the H gates in the state:

\begin{array} { r } { ( | 0 \rangle + | 1 \rangle ) ( | 0 \rangle + | 1 \rangle ) \cdot \cdot \cdot ( | 0 \rangle + | 1 \rangle ) = \sum _ { j _ { 1 } = 0 } ^ { 1 } \cdot \cdot \cdot \sum _ { j _ { t } = 0 } ^ { 1 } | j _ { 1 } \cdot \cdot \cdot j _ { t } \rangle = \sum _ { j = 0 } ^ { 2 ^ { t } - 1 } | j \rangle ) } \end{array}
Thus the initial state of the two registers
is transformed by the circuit as:

\left| 0 \right\rangle | u \rangle \longrightarrow \frac {1}{2 ^ {\frac {t}{2}}} \sum_ {j = 0} ^ {2 ^ {t} - 1} e ^ {2 \pi i \varphi j} | j \rangle | u \rangle = \frac {1}{2 ^ {\frac {t}{2}}} \sum_ {j = 0} ^ {2 ^ {t} - 1} e ^ {2 \pi i \frac {(2 ^ {t} \varphi) j}{2 ^ {t}}} | j \rangle | u \rangle \tag {21.5}
can be expressed exactly with t bits:
(remember that the phase as defined in (21.1) is a real number between 0 and 1).
Consider the eigenvalue equation for a unitary operator
an algorithm to find the phase
, assuming that black boxes (circuits) are available
The procedure of phase estimation uses two registers: the first register contains t
qubits, each in the initial state
. The number t will depend on:
- the accuracy we want to reach in the estimation of the phase
- the probability of success of the algorithm
The second register contains initially the eigenstate
number of qubits necessary to encode the eigenstate.
The circuit for the phase estimation is given in Fig. 21.1. The e↵ect of the
sequence of controlled-U gates on the state

\left| j _ {1} \dots j _ {t} \right\rangle | u \rangle \longrightarrow \left| j _ {1} \dots j _ {t} \right\rangle U ^ {2 ^ {t - 1} j _ {1}} \dots U ^ {2 ^ {0} j _ {t}} | u \rangle = | j \rangle U ^ {j} | u \rangle \tag {21.2}
\left| j \right\rangle \left| u \right\rangle \longrightarrow \left| j \right\rangle e ^ {2 \pi i \varphi j} \left| u \right\rangle \tag {21.3}
The first register exits the H gates in the state:

\begin{array} { r } { ( | 0 \rangle + | 1 \rangle ) ( | 0 \rangle + | 1 \rangle ) \cdot \cdot \cdot ( | 0 \rangle + | 1 \rangle ) = \sum _ { j _ { 1 } = 0 } ^ { 1 } \cdot \cdot \cdot \sum _ { j _ { t } = 0 } ^ { 1 } | j _ { 1 } \cdot \cdot \cdot j _ { t } \rangle = \sum _ { j = 0 } ^ { 2 ^ { t } - 1 } | j \rangle ) } \end{array}
Thus the initial state of the two registers
is transformed by the circuit as:

\left| 0 \right\rangle | u \rangle \longrightarrow \frac {1}{2 ^ {\frac {t}{2}}} \sum_ {j = 0} ^ {2 ^ {t} - 1} e ^ {2 \pi i \varphi j} | j \rangle | u \rangle = \frac {1}{2 ^ {\frac {t}{2}}} \sum_ {j = 0} ^ {2 ^ {t} - 1} e ^ {2 \pi i \frac {(2 ^ {t} \varphi) j}{2 ^ {t}}} | j \rangle | u \rangle \tag {21.5}
can be expressed exactly with t bits:
(remember that the phase as defined in (21.1) is a real number between 0 and 1).
and measuring the register in the computational basis yields the phase

```mermaid
graph LR
  subgraph t_qubits["t qubits"]
    direction TB
  A["H"] --> B["......"]
    C["......"]
    D["......"]
  end

  subgraph u_u["|u⟩[\"|u⟩\"]"]
  E["U2^0"] --> F["......"]
  G["U2^1"] --> H["......"]
  I["U2^2"] --> J["......"]
  K["U2^{t-1}"] --> L["......"]
  end

  style A fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style C fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style D fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style E fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style F fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style G fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style H fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style I fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style J fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style K fill:#fff,stroke:#333
  style L fill:#fff,stroke:#333
```
Fig. 21.1 The circuit for phase estimation.
and measuring the register in the computational basis yields the phase
